steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks which choice emphasizes the location of the figures in The Space in Which We Travel. The notes say the two young girls float "in the center of the canvas" — that spatial detail is what you need to match.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — At 84 by 144 inches, Rawles's The Space in Which We Travel is even larger than the sizable 24-by-36-inch painting Between Us.: describes the paintings' dimensions, not where the figures are. ✗
  • Choice B — While the number of figures may differ, constant among Rawles's hyperrealistic works is the placid mood that the paintings evoke.: conveys feeling or atmosphere, not location. ✗
  • Choice C — In Rawles's painting The Space in Which We Travel, two young girls are depicted in the center of the canvas.: directly names a position on the canvas — a location detail. ✓
  • Choice D — Rawles captures the water in paintings such as Between Us and The Space in Which We Travel in vivid hues of ultramarine and midnight blue.: describes a visual quality, not where the figures are placed. ✗
That is the correct answer.
